tools.name: gn_explore
tools.description:
Concept-level discovery: given a free-text query, returns a structured ExploreReport with top processes, top symbols (with optional skeletons and citation paths), cluster info, and suggested entry points.

Use as the first tool when exploring an unfamiliar concept or feature area.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

query.description: Free-text concept or feature query (e.g. "auth flow", "worker-pool").

depth.description: Controls how many top symbols are returned. shallow=3, balanced=5 (default), deep=10.

qualityMode.description: Search quality vs speed trade-off. Default: balanced.

includeSkeletons.description: Include file skeletons for each top symbol. Default: true.

includeCitations.description: Include graph-path citation edges for each top symbol. Default: true.

tools.intent: Help me understand this concept
tools.whenToUse: First exploration of unfamiliar code

tools.name: gn_explain_module
tools.description:
File/module overview: given a file path, returns exported symbols, cluster membership, co-changed files, last-commit date, and file stats — all in one call.

Use when you need to understand what a file does and how it fits into the codebase.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

filePath.description: Relative or absolute path to the file (e.g. "ontoindex/src/core/search/per-intent-ensemble.ts").

includeSkeleton.description: Include a text skeleton of the file. Default: true.

includePublicAPI.description: Include the list of exported symbols. Default: true.

includeCoChange.description: Include co-changed file partners from git history. Default: true.

recentTouchDays.description: Window in days for "recently touched" classification. Default: 30.

tools.intent: What does this file do?
tools.whenToUse: Need overview of a specific file

tools.name: gn_find_related
tools.description:
Symbol-level neighborhood: given a symbol name or canonical nodeId, returns callers, callees, co-changed files, cluster siblings, and optionally cross-repo references.

Use to explore the blast radius or call graph around a specific function or class.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

symbol.description: Canonical nodeId (e.g. "Function:mergeWithRRF") or fuzzy symbol name (e.g. "mergeWithRRF").

includeCoChanged.description: Include co-changed file partners. Default: true.

includeClusterSiblings.description: Include other symbols in the same Leiden community. Default: true.

includeCrossRepo.description: Include cross-repo references (requires group config). Default: false.

maxItemsPerCategory.description: Maximum items returned per category (callers, callees, etc). Default: 10.

tools.intent: What is near this symbol?
tools.whenToUse: Symbol-level neighborhood

tools.name: gn_safe_edit_check
tools.description:
Pre-edit risk synthesis: resolves a symbol, computes blast radius (callers, callees, processes, clusters), test coverage likelihood, and co-change recency, then emits a SAFE / CAUTION / DANGEROUS / BLOCKED verdict with a recommended tool and suggested next steps.

Run this before modifying any symbol to avoid surprise regressions.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

symbol.description: Canonical nodeId (e.g. "Function:mergeWithRRF") or fuzzy symbol name (e.g. "mergeWithRRF").

intent.description: Type of edit planned. Influences verdict thresholds. Default: general.

force.description: Bypass BLOCKED verdict guards. Use only when you have confirmed the risk manually. Default: false.

docsEvidence.description: Opt in to advisory Markdown docs evidence for related requirements, API specs, and route drift. Does not affect verdict/risk scoring. Default: false.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Is it safe to edit this?
tools.whenToUse: Before any edit (replaces ontoindex_impact)

tools.name: gn_can_delete
tools.description:
Dead-code safety check: resolves a symbol, then checks callers, test-file imports, cross-repo references, and co-change recency to synthesise a DELETE-SAFE / CAUTION / DO-NOT-DELETE verdict.

Run before removing a symbol to confirm it has no live dependencies.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

symbol.description: Canonical nodeId (e.g. "Function:mergeWithRRF") or fuzzy symbol name (e.g. "mergeWithRRF").

includeCrossRepo.description: Check cross-repo references (requires group config). Default: false.

tools.intent: Can I delete this?
tools.whenToUse: Dead-code check

tools.name: gn_pre_commit_audit
tools.description:
Ship-readiness verdict: diffs the working tree, identifies changed symbols, runs per-symbol impact analysis, and emits READY / REVIEW / DO-NOT-COMMIT with a per-file breakdown and any unexpected symbol warnings.

Run before every commit to catch high-risk changes before they land.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

scope.description: Which changes to audit. staged = git diff --cached; unstaged = git diff; all = both; branch = all commits since main. Default: staged.

expectedSymbols.description: Symbols you intended to change. Unexpected changed symbols outside this list are flagged as warnings.

docsEvidence.description: Opt in to advisory Markdown docs evidence for related requirements, API specs, and route drift. Does not affect verdict/risk scoring. Default: false.

tools.intent: Is this commit ready to ship?
tools.whenToUse: Before commit (replaces ontoindex_detect_changes)

tools.name: gn_safe_refactor
tools.description:
Single WRITE dispatcher for atomic refactor operations (rename, modify-body, extract, move). Wraps each operation with symbol resolution, pre-edit safety check (via gn_safe_edit_check), dry-run preview, optional apply, and post-write verification guidance (gn_verify_diff / gn_test_gap).

Defaults to dryRun: true — pass dryRun: false to actually apply changes.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

symbol.description: Preferred symbol selector: canonical nodeId (e.g. "Function:mergeWithRRF") or fuzzy symbol name (e.g. "mergeWithRRF").

target.description: Deprecated alias for symbol, preserved for callers migrating from facade-style target selectors.

params.description: Operation-specific parameters: newName (rename/extract), newBody (modify-body), sourceLineRange (extract), targetFile (move/extract).

dryRun.description: Preview changes without applying. Default: true. Pass false to apply changes.

force.description: Override BLOCKED/DANGEROUS pre-check verdict. Use only after manual confirmation. Default: false.

preChecks.description: Run gn_safe_edit_check before proceeding. Default: true.

tools.intent: Apply rename/extract/move/modify safely
tools.whenToUse: Any refactor (single dispatcher for 6 atomic tools; defaults to dryRun:true)

tools.name: gn_ensure_fresh
tools.description:
Index lifecycle helper: reports whether the OntoIndex index is stale (indexed commit ≠ current HEAD), surfaces embeddings status, and optionally re-runs `ontoindex analyze` using the current CLI process when autoAnalyze: true is passed.

This is a READ-ONLY super-function by default (autoAnalyze defaults to false).

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

withEmbeddings.description: Also check and populate embeddings. Default: false.

autoAnalyze.description: Automatically run ontoindex analyze when the index is stale. Default: false.

killMcpForLock.description: Advisory only: report lock-release guidance before analyzing. OntoIndex will not terminate MCP processes. Default: false.

tools.intent: Make sure the index is current
tools.whenToUse: Before retrieval-heavy ops if repo edited recently

tools.name: gn_quality_mode
tools.description:
Env-var preset switch: applies one of three named quality presets (fast / balanced / thorough) by setting or clearing ONTOINDEX_* environment variables on process.env. Changes take effect immediately for all subsequent tool calls in the same session.

fast: clears all flags (fastest). balanced: enables INTENT_ENSEMBLE + CITATIONS. thorough: balanced + LSP_REFERENCES + VEC_POOL_MIN=3.

duration.description: Advisory only — both values set process.env for the lifetime of the process. Default: session.

tools.intent: Set retrieval quality preset
tools.whenToUse: At session start (fast/balanced/thorough)

tools.name: gn_diff_impact
tools.description:
PR blast-radius report: diffs the working tree (staged, branch, or an explicit commit range), finds symbols defined in each changed file, runs upstream/downstream impact analysis per symbol, aggregates HIGH-risk symbols, and optionally suggests reviewers from git history.

Use before opening a PR or after a large feature branch to understand the full blast radius of your changes.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

commitRange.description: Explicit git commit range (e.g. "HEAD~5..HEAD", "main...feature"). Omit to use staged changes.

scope.description: Which changes to diff. staged = git diff --cached; branch = main...HEAD; commit-range requires commitRange param. Default: staged.

includeReviewers.description: Suggest reviewers from git blame/log history. Default: true.

docsEvidence.description: Opt in to advisory Markdown docs evidence for related requirements, API specs, and route drift. Does not affect risk reporting. Default: false.

tools.intent: What is the blast radius of these commits? (MCP surface)
tools.whenToUse: Commit-range or staged blast-radius analysis via MCP; pair with `ontoindex review diff` for local CLI review. Hosted PR adapter is a later Phase 6 feature.

tools.name: gn_review_diff
tools.description:
Graph-aware diff review with ADR 0018 capability-response envelope.

Diffs the working tree (staged, branch, or an explicit commit range), finds symbols defined in each changed file, runs upstream/downstream impact analysis per symbol, and returns the result in the same versioned envelope as `ontoindex review diff --json`.

Use when you want the MCP response to be machine-readable and aligned with the CLI review contract. Use `gn_diff_impact` when you also need reviewer suggestions or docs evidence.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

commitRange.description: Explicit git commit range (e.g. "HEAD~5..HEAD", "main...feature"). Omit to use staged changes.

scope.description: Which changes to diff. staged = git diff --cached; branch = main...HEAD; commit-range requires commitRange param. Default: staged.

tools.intent: Return graph-aware diff review in the ADR 0018 envelope
tools.whenToUse: Machine-readable local diff review aligned with ontoindex review diff --json

tools.name: gn_diagnose
tools.description:
Read-only system-status report: checks index freshness, embeddings, LSP server availability, and ONTOINDEX_* environment variables, then synthesises a ranked recommendation list.

Use when the OntoIndex index seems stale, searches return poor results, or you want a health snapshot before a long session.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

checkLsp.description: Probe whether typescript-language-server, pyright, and rust-analyzer are on PATH. Default: true.

checkEmbeddings.description: Check whether embeddings are populated. Default: true.

checkIndexFreshness.description: Check whether the index is stale vs the current HEAD. Default: true.

checkToolContract.description: Check whether gn_help advertised tools match registered callable MCP tools. Default: true.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: What is not optimal in my OntoIndex setup?
tools.whenToUse: When something feels off; session-start health check

tools.name: gn_propose_location
tools.description:
Where-to-add-new-code suggester: given a free-text intent description, uses semantic search to find the best-matching clusters, then proposes a directory, filename, and import pattern for the new code.

Use before creating a new file to find the right location and naming convention.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

intent.description: Free-text description of the new code (e.g. "test feature handler", "auth middleware").

language.description: Target language for the file extension suggestion (e.g. "python" → .py; anything else → .ts). Default: ts.

tools.intent: Where should I add new code for X?
tools.whenToUse: When adding new code; cluster-aware placement suggestion

tools.name: gn_help
tools.description: Compact startup guide and MCP ergonomics review: lists super-functions, docs-aware workflows, setup steps, response-size limits, schema clarity notes, and recommended first calls for agents.

topic.description: Optional discovery focus. Current response is compact and includes all topics; use this to document caller intent.

mode.description: Optional agent mode. When supplied, filters advertised tools and workflow guidance to the specified mode and adds mode/modeDescription to the report.

query.description: Optional free-text discovery query. Filters/ranks tools by registry intent/whenToUse/workflow tags.

evidenceClass.description: Optional evidence-class filter. By default, advisory_memory/runtime_diagnostic are excluded unless includeNonAuthoritativeEvidence=true.

evidenceClasses.description: Optional multi-value evidence-class filter (same vocabulary as evidenceClass).

stability.description: Optional stability filter for registry-backed discovery results.

includeNonAuthoritativeEvidence.description: Include advisory_memory/runtime_diagnostic evidence classes in filtering. Defaults to false to preserve trust boundaries.

limit.description: Optional cap for filtered results (min 1, max 100).

repo.description: Optional repository name or path. When supplied, adds lightweight readiness reminders (stale index, dirty worktree, missing embeddings/LSP/sidecar) to the report.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: List all super-functions
tools.whenToUse: First call when discovering the surface

tools.name: gn_docs
tools.description: Docs-specific safe agent reports over stabilized docs JSON contracts: trace requirements, check API drift, inspect docs context/readiness, and return compact MCP-safe metadata with skip reasons.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

includeMemories.description: Opt in to advisory memory summary metadata for action="context" or "readiness". Ignored for trace/drift and never used as docs evidence or readiness authority.

maxItems.description: Maximum compact docs evidence items to return.

limit.description: Alias for maxItems, for consistency with other bounded MCP tools.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous docs response. Keeps deterministic page boundaries for follow-up pages.

summary.description: Return lighter JSON that keeps status, freshness, and warnings while omitting heavy nested evidence.

minimal.description: Return only the core result summary and next action.

maxCandidatesPerFact.description: Maximum ambiguous candidates retained per docs evidence fact.

format.description: Optional derived formatter. Omitted/json returns canonical compact JSON only; inline/both also include inlineContext text derived from the JSON report.

maxTokens.description: Maximum estimated tokens for inlineContext when format is inline or both.

maxEvidenceItems.description: Maximum evidence bullets included in inlineContext.

tools.intent: Inspect docs trace, drift, context, or readiness
tools.whenToUse: When you need bounded docs evidence without raw graph queries; use includeMemories only for advisory context/readiness and never as trace/drift evidence.

tools.name: gn_tool_contract
tools.description: MCP tool contract preflight: compare gn_help advertised super-functions with the registered callable MCP frontier and report missing or extra tools before agents hit Unknown tool at runtime. When `mode` is supplied, also computes a mode-filtered frontier comparison and structural integrity checks.

includeFacades.description: Include facade tools such as audit, inspect, impact, and search in callable output. Default: false.

mode.description: Optional agent mode. When supplied, adds a mode-filtered frontier comparison (`modeFrontier`) to the report comparing gn_help({mode}) advertised tools against mode-discoverable callable tools.

tools.intent: Verify gn_help advertised tools match the registered callable MCP frontier
tools.whenToUse: At session start or after Unknown tool errors to detect stale MCP/runtime drift

tools.name: gn_audit_ingest
tools.description: Audit lifecycle ingest: parse a Markdown report or pasted findings into untrusted candidate findings at a locked target HEAD. Ingest never creates OPEN findings.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

report.description: Audit report path. Alias for sourcePath.

target.description: Target git ref to lock. Alias for targetRef. Default: HEAD.

targetRef.description: Target git ref to lock. Default: HEAD.

graphIndexId.description: Optional graph index identity to attach to candidates.

persist.description: Persist ingest events to .ontoindex/audit. Default: true.

tools.intent: Ingest an audit report as candidate findings
tools.whenToUse: Before verifying or bundling audit findings; never emits OPEN

tools.name: gn_audit_verify
tools.description: Audit lifecycle verify: re-check candidate findings against fresh target HEAD evidence and classify unsupported or incomplete proof without promoting stale findings to OPEN.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

session.description: Audit session id. Alias for sessionId.

finding.description: Inline lifecycle finding object to verify instead of loading a session.

maxFindings.description: Maximum findings verified in one response. Default: 25, max: 100.

maxEvidence.description: Maximum evidence items per finding. Default: 25, max: 100.

persist.description: Persist verification/status events when a session is supplied. Default: true.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Verify candidate findings against target HEAD
tools.whenToUse: Before accepting OPEN or RESOLVED audit status

tools.name: gn_fix_history
tools.description: Audit lifecycle fix-history lookup: search git history at target HEAD for commits matching supplied fix or negative-evidence patterns.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

targetHead.description: Locked target commit to search from.

patterns.description: Git -G patterns to search in history.

tools.intent: Find commits that may have fixed a finding
tools.whenToUse: When a finding may be stale or already resolved

tools.name: gn_audit_bundle
tools.description: Audit lifecycle bundle projection: group verified OPEN/PARTIAL findings into bounded implementation bundles. No dispatch prompts are generated.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

session.description: Audit session id. Alias for sessionId.

tools.intent: Group verified findings into implementation bundles
tools.whenToUse: After verification and linting, before implementation planning

tools.name: gn_audit_lint
tools.description: Audit lifecycle lint: report or bundle process checks for stale OPEN findings, line-only evidence, runtime-only claims, duplicates, tombstones, HOLD metadata, tests, and impact targets.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

session.description: Audit session id. Alias for sessionId.

scope.description: Rule set to run. Default: report.

advisory.description: Recommend zero exit even when issues exist. Default: false.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous lint response. Keeps deterministic page boundaries for follow-up pages.

summary.description: Return lighter JSON that keeps status and warnings while omitting bulky per-item detail.

minimal.description: Return only the core result summary and next action.

maxIssues.description: Maximum lint issues returned. Default: 50, max: 100.

includeIgnored.description: Include findings whose target paths match repository ignore/generated policy. Default: false.

persist.description: Persist lint event when a session is supplied. Default: true.

tools.intent: Check audit quality gates
tools.whenToUse: Before accepting an audit report or bundle set

tools.name: gn_audit_logic
tools.description: Systems-audit logic scan: run bounded deterministic anti-pattern rules for resources, fork safety, signals, TOCTOU, and concurrency. Findings are evidence only and do not directly change audit lifecycle status.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

path.description: Repository-relative source path or snippet label to scan.

source.description: Optional source text to scan directly.

category.description: Rule category to run. Omit to run all MVP categories.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Scan for systems anti-pattern evidence
tools.whenToUse: When auditing resources, fork safety, signals, TOCTOU, or concurrency

tools.name: gn_audit_dedupe
tools.description: Audit lifecycle dedupe: group findings by exact fingerprint, symbol, root cause, write-set, or test surface so stale duplicate audit claims collapse before dispatch.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

tools.intent: Collapse duplicate findings by root cause, write-set, symbol, or test surface
tools.whenToUse: Before bundling noisy audit reports or re-emitted stale claims

tools.name: gn_dispatch_prompt
tools.description: Audit lifecycle dispatch prompt generator: emit one concrete worker prompt for exactly one verified implementation bundle, with scope, non-scope, tests, impact checks, and stop conditions.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

bundleId.description: Bundle id to dispatch. Required when the session has more than one bundle.

forbidUnverifiedFindings.description: Reject bundles without fresh verified implementation findings. Default: true.

allowRuntimeOnlyFindings.description: Allow runtime-only findings to be dispatched. Default: false.

tools.intent: Generate one concrete worker prompt for one verified bundle
tools.whenToUse: After bundling and before assigning implementation work

tools.name: gn_audit_tombstone_create
tools.description: Audit lifecycle tombstone creation: mark a resolved finding as tombstoned with negative/fix-proof evidence so future stale audit reports can be rejected.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

reason.description: Why this finding must not be reopened without invariant failure.

tools.intent: Record fixed findings that must not be reopened while invariants hold
tools.whenToUse: After a verified fix or RESOLVED-ALREADY classification

tools.name: gn_audit_session_start
tools.description: Manager-level audit session start: ingest findings and create the session lock that governs the rest of the audit loop.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

targetRef.description: Target git ref to ingest against. Default: HEAD.

graphIndexId.description: Optional graph index identity to attach to candidates.

strictFresh.description: Advisory manager preference for strict freshness handling. Default: true.

persist.description: Persist ingest events and session lock. Default: true.

tools.intent: Start the governed manager loop by ingesting findings and creating a session lock
tools.whenToUse: Preferred first step for customer-facing audit work

tools.name: gn_audit_session_verify
tools.description: Manager-level audit verify: refuse stale sessions, run fresh verification, and enforce repeated-finding tombstones before work can proceed.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

proofMode.description: Advisory proof-mode label for the manager loop.

maxFindings.description: Maximum findings verified in one response. Default: 25.

maxEvidence.description: Maximum evidence items per finding. Default: 25.

persist.description: Persist verification and repeated-finding tombstone events. Default: true.

tools.intent: Verify a locked audit session and enforce repeated-finding tombstones before work
tools.whenToUse: Preferred verification step in the manager loop

tools.name: gn_audit_session_dedupe
tools.description: Manager-level audit dedupe: refuse stale sessions, then collapse duplicates before implementation planning or dispatch.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

tools.intent: Collapse duplicates inside the locked manager loop
tools.whenToUse: Before manager bundling or dispatch

tools.name: gn_audit_session_bundle
tools.description: Manager-level audit bundle: run dedupe first, then project verified findings into implementation bundles with optional manager sizing limits.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

maxLoc.description: Optional manager limit for estimated lines changed per bundle.

maxFiles.description: Optional manager limit for files touched per bundle.

tools.intent: Run dedupe first, then create implementation bundles in the manager loop
tools.whenToUse: Preferred bundling step before dispatch

tools.name: gn_audit_session_dispatch
tools.description: Manager-level audit dispatch: refuse stale sessions, unverified findings, HOLD/NEEDS-VERIFY statuses, and duplicate-only bundle children before generating a worker prompt.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

tools.intent: Generate a worker prompt only for a persisted, dispatchable manager bundle
tools.whenToUse: Preferred worker handoff path after manager bundling

tools.name: gn_audit_session_review_worker
tools.description: Manager-level audit worker review: run scope guard and required-test checks against a persisted bundle after worker edits.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

changedFiles.description: Changed files from the implementation diff.

changedSymbols.description: Changed symbols from the implementation diff.

requiredTests.description: Override required tests for this review.

tools.intent: Review worker edits with scope guard and required test checks
tools.whenToUse: Preferred post-worker manager review step

tools.name: gn_audit_session_lock
tools.description: Audit lifecycle session lock: create, load, or validate a hard audit session lock containing target HEAD, graph index/hash, OntoIndex version, and tombstone snapshot. Validation returns STALE_SESSION on drift.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

currentHead.description: Override current HEAD for validation tests.

graphIndexId.description: Override current graph index id for validation.

graphHash.description: Override current graph hash for creation/validation.

ontoindexVersion.description: Override OntoIndex version recorded in the lock.

tools.intent: Create or validate a hard HEAD/graph/tombstone lock for an audit session
tools.whenToUse: Before lifecycle work and whenever current HEAD or graph freshness may have drifted

tools.name: gn_audit_pr_marker_scan
tools.description: Audit lifecycle PR marker scan: inspect comments around evidence lines for PR-N, TODO, FIXME, follow-up, known limitation, and deferred markers before reflagging known debt as bugs.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

path.description: Repository-relative source path or inline source label.

tools.intent: Find nearby PR/TODO/FIXME/follow-up/deferred markers around evidence
tools.whenToUse: Before reflagging code that may already be known deferred debt or decision-gated work

tools.name: gn_audit_diff
tools.description: Audit lifecycle diff: compare two persisted audit sessions by finding fingerprint/id and report added, removed, status-changed, and unchanged findings.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

maxEntries.description: Maximum entries per diff bucket. Default: 100.

tools.intent: Show semantic changes between two audit sessions
tools.whenToUse: When comparing audit rounds or producing an audit changelog

tools.name: gn_audit_replay
tools.description: Audit lifecycle replay planner: replay a session against a target HEAD by returning findings that need verify/reverify because status, target HEAD, or evidence freshness changed.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

targetHead.description: Target HEAD to replay against. Default: current git HEAD.

tools.intent: Plan re-verification of a prior session against a newer target HEAD
tools.whenToUse: When avoiding regenerated stale Markdown audits after code has changed

tools.name: gn_audit_export
tools.description: Audit lifecycle export: produce canonical JSON and/or generated Markdown from a persisted audit session so agents do not manually regenerate stale prose reports.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

tools.intent: Export canonical audit session JSON or generated Markdown
tools.whenToUse: When producing audit artifacts without hand-written stale prose reports

tools.name: gn_scope_guard
tools.description: Audit lifecycle scope guard: compare an implementation diff summary against a bundle write-set, symbols, required tests, and neighboring bundles.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

changedFiles.description: Changed files from the implementation diff.

changedSymbols.description: Changed symbols from the implementation diff.

executedTests.description: Tests actually run by the worker.

requiredTests.description: Override required tests for this guard run.

tools.intent: Check whether a worker stayed inside bundle scope
tools.whenToUse: During manager review of an implementation diff

tools.name: gn_bundle_conflicts
tools.description: Audit lifecycle bundle conflict detector: report file, symbol, test-surface, and write-set overlaps before parallel worker dispatch.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

bundleIds.description: Optional bundle ids to filter conflict output.

strategy.description: Bundle grouping strategy to evaluate. Default: root-cause.

tools.intent: Detect write-set, symbol, file, and test-surface conflicts between bundles
tools.whenToUse: Before dispatching bundles in parallel

tools.name: gn_verify_diff
tools.description: Post-edit diff verification: compare expected files, symbols, and tests against actual changed files, changed symbols, impacted symbols, and executed tests.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

scope.description: Diff scope to inspect. Default: unstaged.

diffRef.description: Optional compare base ref. When present, gn_verify_diff uses compare mode.

tools.intent: Compare expected scope against actual changed files, symbols, impact, and tests
tools.whenToUse: After edits when detect_changes alone is too vague

tools.name: gn_test_gap
tools.description: Post-edit test evidence review: report changed production symbols that have no linked tests or executed test evidence. Filename-derived matches remain heuristic until richer test data is ingested.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

scope.description: Diff scope to inspect. Default: unstaged.

diffRef.description: Optional compare base ref. When present, gn_test_gap uses compare mode.

tools.intent: Report changed production symbols with missing test evidence
tools.whenToUse: After edits to confirm changed code still has linked or executed tests

tools.name: gn_worker_scope_review
tools.description: Write-through worker review: validate a bundle against changed files, changed symbols, impacted symbols, executed tests, and missing test evidence after worker edits.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

commit.description: Optional compare base ref or commit for diff collection.

changedFiles.description: Changed files from the implementation diff.

changedSymbols.description: Changed symbols from the implementation diff.

executedTests.description: Tests actually run by the worker.

requiredTests.description: Override required tests for this review.

tools.intent: Run write-through review for a dispatched bundle after worker edits
tools.whenToUse: Preferred explicit worker verification step after bundle edits

tools.name: gn_resource_trace
tools.description: Systems-audit resource ownership trace: extract POSIX resource acquire/duplicate/handoff/release facts for fd, pid, pidfd, pipe, socket, fork, exec, and wait flows.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Trace POSIX resource ownership acquire/duplicate/release facts
tools.whenToUse: When proving or rejecting fd, pid, pidfd, pipe, socket, fork, exec, or wait lifecycle claims

tools.name: gn_path_verify
tools.description: Systems-audit shallow path verifier: for a trigger branch, verify required calls/patterns appear and forbidden calls/patterns do not appear in the bounded intra-procedural window.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

when.description: Trigger condition or branch pattern, e.g. fork() < 0.

must.description: Patterns that must appear after the trigger.

mustNot.description: Patterns that must not appear after the trigger.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Verify shallow branch/path invariants
tools.whenToUse: When a finding depends on a specific branch such as fork failure or MSG_CTRUNC handling

tools.name: gn_test_suggestions
tools.description: Audit-to-test suggestion generator: propose the smallest test file/case/assertion shape for a verified finding, symbol, claim pattern, or risk invariant.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

claimPattern.description: Claim pattern or invariant under test.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Suggest the smallest audit regression test shape
tools.whenToUse: For a verified OPEN finding that needs test evidence before dispatch

tools.name: gn_trace_boundary
tools.description: Systems-audit resource boundary trace: trace FD/resource handoff across SCM_RIGHTS, pidfd_getfd, fork inheritance, and exec close-on-exec filtering without using FD number equality as identity proof.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

resource.description: Resource kind to trace, for example fd or signal_mask.

start.description: Starting symbol, file, or source-side handle label.

end.description: Optional expected destination symbol, file, or receiver label.

mechanism.description: Boundary handoff mechanism. Omit to infer from evidence.

source.description: Optional source text to trace directly.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Trace resource handoff across process boundaries
tools.whenToUse: When following FD or process-boundary resource lifecycle evidence

tools.name: gn_extract_fsm
tools.description: Systems-audit FSM extraction: map enum/state assignments, transition guards, and missing-state guard warnings from bounded source text or a source path.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Extract state machines from enums and state variables
tools.whenToUse: When state transitions or missing guards are spread across a class/module

tools.name: gn_error_topology
tools.description: Systems-audit error topology: find errno/exception/error-return sources, checks, sinks, swallowed errors, and generic exit-code black holes.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Map error sources, sinks, and swallowed failures
tools.whenToUse: When auditing errno, exceptions, generic exits, or silent catch blocks

tools.name: gn_concurrency_audit
tools.description: Systems-audit concurrency scan: identify locks, lock scopes, blocking or allocation work under locks, nested locks, and possible lock-order inversion.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Find lock-scope hazards and lock-order risks
tools.whenToUse: When auditing mutex contention, blocking calls under locks, or inversion risk

tools.name: gn_pressure_impact
tools.description: Systems-audit pressure impact: model global quota/active-count/max-concurrent constraints and report ALL/global side-effect warnings.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Find global quota and saturation side effects
tools.whenToUse: When a symbol changes active counts, quotas, or max-concurrent constraints

tools.name: gn_taint_trace
tools.description: Systems-audit taint trace: bounded source-to-sink data-flow heuristic with sanitizer detection and provenance-backed findings.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

maxPaths.description: Maximum taint paths returned. Default: 25, max: 100.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Trace bounded untrusted source-to-sink data flow
tools.whenToUse: When checking whether input reaches a dangerous sink without sanitization

tools.name: gn_abi_diff
tools.description: Systems-audit ABI diff: compare C++/Rust/JSON source payloads with TypeScript/JSON targets and flag precision, nullability, and field mismatches.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

sourceStruct.description: Inline source struct or JSON payload.

sourcePath.description: Path to source struct or payload.

targetInterface.description: Inline target TypeScript interface or JSON payload.

targetPath.description: Path to target interface or payload.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Compare cross-language payload and interface shapes
tools.whenToUse: When C++/Rust/JSON payloads may not match TypeScript/JSON consumers

tools.name: gn_simulate_fault
tools.description: Systems-audit semantic fault simulation: statically model a target call returning a chosen value and report likely branches, assignments, early returns, and bypass warnings.

repo.description: Repository name or path. Omit if only one repo is indexed.

target.description: Target call to force, e.g. pidfd_open.

legacyResponse.description: Return the legacy pre-envelope response shape. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope.

tools.intent: Simulate a target call returning a fault value
tools.whenToUse: When reasoning about ENOSYS, failure returns, bypasses, and fallback paths

tools.name: discover
tools.description: Discover repositories, routes, tools, and analysis packs.

repo.description: Repository name or path.

tools.intent: Discover repositories, routes, tools, and analysis packs
tools.whenToUse: When you need to find high-level project components or capabilities

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search the knowledge graph using semantic, Cypher, or repomap queries. Semantic search can opt in to sidecar enrichment, passive related facts, Markdown context, and Markdown PPR metadata.

repo.description: Repository name or path.

service.description: Optional monorepo service root. In group mode (@repo), prefix-matches member file paths.

query.description: Search query or Cypher statement. For action="semantic", this can also carry the existing typed-query document when typed_query is true.

typed_query.description: Parse query as the existing typed-query document when action="semantic". Current degraded capabilities: @group searches fall back to plain semantic search, vec/hyde lanes downgrade when embeddings are unavailable, and graph lanes may fall back to BM25 seeds when traversal is unavailable.

structured_output.description: For action="semantic", include structured_retrieval candidates, evidence references, and capability state when the backend can produce them. Default: false.

task_context.description: What you are working on. Helps semantic ranking.

goal.description: What you want to find. Helps semantic ranking.

include_content.description: Include full symbol source content for semantic results.

include_skeleton.description: Include AST skeletons for top semantic result files.

consume_enrichment_facts.description: Opt in to sidecar enrichment facts under the top-level enrichment envelope.

retrieval_policy.description: Named retrieval expansion policy. Defaults to graph-only behavior unless explicitly set.

include_passive_related_facts.description: Opt in to HippoRAG-style passive related fact metadata when consume_enrichment_facts is true.

include_markdown_context.description: Include Markdown document context when enrichment facts and passive related facts are enabled.

include_markdown_ppr.description: Include bounded Markdown document-only PPR metadata with Markdown context.

focus.description: Repomap focus file paths or symbol names.

tools.intent: Search the knowledge graph using semantic, Cypher, or repomap queries
tools.whenToUse: When you need to find specific symbols, patterns, or files via the graph

tools.name: inspect
tools.description: Inspect symbol context, evidence packs, API shapes, or IPC traces. Context inspection can opt in to sidecar enrichment metadata.

repo.description: Repository name or path.

service.description: Optional monorepo service root. In group mode (@repo), prefix-matches member file paths.

target.description: Facade alias. Maps to name for context, targets[0] for evidence, route for shape, and symbol_name for IPC.

include_content.description: Include full symbol source content in context results.

consume_enrichment_facts.description: Opt in to sidecar enrichment facts under the top-level enrichment envelope.

retrieval_policy.description: Named retrieval expansion policy. Defaults to graph-only behavior unless explicitly set.

include_passive_related_facts.description: Opt in to HippoRAG-style passive related fact metadata when consume_enrichment_facts is true.

include_markdown_context.description: Include Markdown document context when enrichment facts and passive related facts are enabled.

include_markdown_ppr.description: Include bounded Markdown document-only PPR metadata with Markdown context.

neighborhood_mode.description: Explicit bounded context neighborhood mode. When omitted, context keeps the default symbol view.

targets.description: Evidence-pack targets such as file:line or symbol names.

tools.intent: Inspect symbol context, evidence packs, API shapes, or IPC traces
tools.whenToUse: When you need deep context or evidence for a specific symbol or route

tools.name: impact
tools.description: Analyze impact of changes on symbols, routes, or batches of symbols. Symbol impact supports opt-in sidecar enrichment with a safety-critical gate.

action.description: The impact analysis action to perform.

repo.description: Repository name or path.

service.description: Optional monorepo service root. In group mode (@repo), prefix-matches member file paths.

target.description: Symbol name, UID, route, or batch seed. Facade maps route target to route and batch target to targets[0].

target_uid.description: Direct symbol UID for symbol impact.

direction.description: Impact direction for symbol and batch analysis. Defaults to upstream.

includeTests.description: Include test relationships in impact traversal.

consume_enrichment_facts.description: Opt in to sidecar enrichment facts under the top-level enrichment envelope.

allow_safety_critical_enrichment.description: Allow sidecar fact consumption for safety-critical impact analysis.

tools.intent: Analyze impact of changes on symbols, routes, or batches of symbols
tools.whenToUse: When checking the blast radius of a change (pre-edit or post-edit)

tools.name: audit
tools.description: Run architectural audits, manager-level audit session workflows, write-through verification, and systems-audit checks.

repo.description: Repository name or path.

legacyResponse.description: Forward legacy response mode to envelope-capable audit actions. Default: true. Set false to opt into the capability-aware response envelope where supported.

tools.intent: Run architectural audits, session workflows, and systems-audit checks
tools.whenToUse: Primary entry point for the audit lifecycle and systems reasoning

tools.name: refactor
tools.description: Perform safe refactoring: rename symbols, replace bodies, or stage in sandbox.

repo.description: Repository name or path.

target.description: Symbol name or UID alias for rename/replace actions.

tools.intent: Perform safe refactoring: rename symbols, replace bodies, or stage in sandbox
tools.whenToUse: When applying code changes through the refactoring engine

tools.name: manage
tools.description: Manage OntoIndex sessions and internal route maps.
tools.intent: Manage OntoIndex sessions and internal route maps
tools.whenToUse: When performing administrative session or route management

tools.name: docs
tools.description: Docs-specific safe agent tools over stabilized docs JSON contracts. Returns compact typed reports for docs trace, docs drift, docs context, and docs readiness without exposing raw docs graph queries.

repo.description: Repository name or path.

includeMemories.description: Opt in to advisory memory summary metadata for action="context" or "readiness". Ignored for trace/drift and never used as docs evidence or readiness authority.

maxItems.description: Maximum compact docs evidence items to return.

limit.description: Alias for maxItems, for consistency with other bounded MCP tools.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous docs response. Keeps deterministic page boundaries for follow-up pages.

summary.description: Return lighter JSON that keeps status, freshness, and warnings while omitting heavy nested evidence.

minimal.description: Return only the core result summary and next action.

maxCandidatesPerFact.description: Maximum ambiguous candidates retained per docs evidence fact.

format.description: Optional derived formatter. Omitted/json returns canonical compact JSON only; inline/both also include inlineContext text derived from the JSON report.

maxTokens.description: Maximum estimated tokens for inlineContext when format is inline or both.

maxEvidenceItems.description: Maximum evidence bullets included in inlineContext.

tools.intent: Docs-specific safe agent reports for trace, drift, context, and readiness
tools.whenToUse: When reasoning about requirements and documentation coverage
